contract.goal: Add the typed WRITE half of the manifest.toml layer on top of U1/U2: serialize a ManifestToml back to sectioned TOML, and write it atomically with a corruption guard (verify-before-rename) and an optimistic-concurrency guard (mtime/size re-check). LIB ONLY — tested against temp-dir fixtures; the live writer-verb dispatch and the on-disk project conversion both stay in U5 (keeps the dogfood loop running on JSON through U3/U4). The riskiest seam, isolated.
acceptance_criteria: manifest-toml.ts gains stringifyManifest(m: ManifestToml): string (inverse of readManifest; emits the six sections via stringifyToml); honors null-by-absence (meta nullable scalars + undefined optional phase fields omitted; [config] omits schema_version)
round-trip closes: readManifest(stringifyManifest(m)) deep-equals m (null-stripped), pinned vs the U2 real fixture AND synthetic manifests (null current_branch, empty-detail event, phase without pr)

writeManifest(path, m, opts?: { expect?: WriteToken }): WriteToken — serialize, verify-before-rename (stripNullish(readManifest(s)) deep-equals stripNullish(m); throw rather than rename on mismatch), then atomic temp + renameSync

optimistic concurrency: readManifestFile(path) captures WriteToken={mtimeMs,size}; writeManifest with a stale expect token aborts loudly (manifest-changed-under-write) instead of clobbering; fresh write proceeds; + single-writer-per-project note
append-only preservation test: read manifest with N events, append one in-memory, writeManifest, read back -> events toEqual the original N in order plus the new one
strip-only smoke extended (stringifyManifest + writeManifest->readManifestFile to a temp path) under node; standalone exit 0 + marker
vitest green (new + full loom suite) + standalone node smoke exit 0
rules_applied: Lib only: no verb-dispatch edits, no live manifest.json touched, no project conversion (all U5)
Atomic temp+rename (the current writeFileSync is non-atomic — this is net-new safety)

verify-before-rename is compare-to-original null-stripped deep-equal (NOT a fixed-point check, which can't catch a consistently-dropped field); immune to the lenient-detail null/absent asymmetry via stripNullish
loud LoomError on verify failure and on concurrent modification
harvest the temp-dir test idiom (mkdtempSync + rmSync) from loom.test.ts makeCtx; unique temp dir per test (parallel-safe)
disqualifiers: rewiring any verb to the toml write path (U5)
converting any real project's on-disk state (U5)
a verify that uses a fixed-point check instead of compare-to-original
inputs: projects/2026-05-26-substrate-consolidation/whiteboards/2-single-file-toml-state.md (Q3 append-only + atomic-write, verify-before-rename + mtime re-check mandates)

actions: manifest-toml.ts: added stringifyManifest (builds the six-section root, strips null/undefined via stripNullish, drops [config] schema_version via configToSection, emits via stringifyToml); WriteToken type; readManifestFile (fs read + stat-captured token); writeManifest (verify-before-rename via deepEqual(stripNullish(readManifest(s)), stripNullish(m)) -> throw manifest-write-verify-failed; mtime/size re-check when opts.expect set -> throw manifest-changed-under-write; atomic writeFileSync(tmp)+renameSync; returns post-write token); internal stripNullish + deepEqual

manifest-toml-write.test.ts: 9 fs/temp-dir tests — pure round-trip (fixture + synthetic null/empty/no-pr), [config]-omits-schema_version, atomic write (no .tmp left, bytes == stringifyManifest, round-trips), fresh write, optimistic-concurrency loud-abort + current-token-succeeds, append-only order preservation, spawnSync('node') strip-only smoke
manifest-toml.smoke.ts: extended to exercise stringifyManifest round-trip + a real writeManifest->readManifestFile to a temp path under node
Verified: npx vitest run plugins/loom = 16 files / 217 passed; manifest-toml-write.test.ts isolated = 9 passed; node plugins/loom/cli/lib/manifest-toml.smoke.ts exit 0 with marker
Evaluator panel (contract-fit + test-unit + naming) returned approved; one naming ADVISORY (non-gating) on the read/readFile/write naming asymmetry

changes_since_previous: Third unit of Phase 2. Adds the typed write path (stringifyManifest + atomic writeManifest with verify-before-rename + mtime/size concurrency guard) on top of U2's reader. Lib only — U4 folds checkins/sessions through the same mechanism; U5 flips the writer verbs onto this path and converts the on-disk state atomically.
notes_for_pr: Operator decisions this unit (grilled before contract): (1) WRITE-PATH LIB ONLY — the verb-dispatch flip + this/sibling-project conversion both defer to U5, because the source loom does this project's own bookkeeping and it is still JSON until U5 (flipping verbs now would break the dogfood loop on its own project). (2) Concurrency safety = mtime/size re-check before rename -> loud abort, plus a single-writer-per-project note (vs document-only or lockfile).

Design wrinkle resolved: the lenient-detail decision (U2) means Event.detail can carry null (e.g. a research event's topic: string | null), and TOML encodes null as absence — so a naive deep-equal verify would false-fail on null-vs-absent. verify-before-rename therefore compares null-STRIPPED trees (stripNullish), which stays immune to the asymmetry while still catching a dropped non-null field.

verify-before-rename is compare-to-original, NOT a fixed-point check: stringifyManifest(readManifest(s)) === s would pass even if the serializer consistently dropped a field (the dropped field is absent on both sides). Comparing the re-parsed output against the original m catches that class.

Naming ADVISORY (non-gating, kept deliberately): readManifest(string) / readManifestFile(path) / writeManifest(path) is asymmetric. The decision: the ...File suffix exists ONLY to disambiguate readManifest (pure, string) from readManifestFile (fs, path). Since there is no writeManifest(string), the write side needs no suffix — a suffix carrying no disambiguation is noise. The pure pair is readManifest/stringifyManifest; the fs pair is readManifestFile/writeManifest. Suffix-only-where-it-disambiguates is the principle; documented here so the asymmetry reads as deliberate, not sloppy.

Panel = contract-fit + test-unit + naming. Same manual override: derive-panel adds evaluator-react-api for these pure-Node .ts files (no React/JSX) — dropped. Substrate-followup: gate react-api on JSX/react-import presence.
Scope-shift detection: zero signals (panel approved, grill decisions were unit-contract scope, no round-2 whiteboard, U3 is not the phase's last unit). No revise offered.
#73 (Phase 2 U2) pr-opened + pr-merged reconciled into this commit per the per-unit cadence recipe.
